name: escape-from-freedom
description: Erich Fromm's Escape from Freedom — psychoanalytic and social psychology toolkit examining why people flee from the burden of freedom into authoritarianism, conformity, and destructiveness
useCase: Understand the paradox of freedom, identify escape mechanisms, analyze authoritarianism, recognize pseudo-individuality, and achieve genuine positive freedom
knownRisks: Fromm's psychoanalytic framework may not appeal to readers who prefer empirical social science
The book was written in 1941 — some historical examples are specific to that era, even if the principles remain relevant
Fromm's critique of modern democracy can feel overly pessimistic
